input_dict.agent_role: Integrate second-hop evidence with first-hop context and retain the facts needed to answer the question.

task_context.question: Which genus of plant had more species identified with it in the past, Carissa or Aruncus?
task_context.agent_role: Integrate second-hop evidence with first-hop context and retain the facts needed to answer the question.

visible_agent_inputs.question: Which genus of plant had more species identified with it in the past, Carissa or Aruncus?
visible_agent_inputs.context: Carissa — about 100 species were listed in the past, compared with only 1–4 species for Aruncus.
passages: Sophronica carissae | Sophronica carissae is a species of beetle in the family Cerambycidae.  It was described by Fisher in 1930.

Carissa | Carissa is a genus of shrubs or small trees native to tropical and subtropical regions of Africa, Australia and Asia.  Until recently about 100 species were listed, but most of them have been relegated to the status of synonyms or assigned to other genera, such as "Acokanthera".

Carissa spinarum | Carissa spinarum, the conkerberry or bush plum, is a large shrub of the dogbane family (Apocynaceae), widely distributed in tropical regions of Africa, Southern Asia, Australia, and various islands of the Indian Ocean.  It is most well known in Australia, where it is also called "currant bush" or, more ambiguously, "native currant" or even "black currant".  It is, however, neither closely related to plums ("Prunus") nor to true currants ("Ribes"), which belong to entirely different lineages of eudicots.  In India, it is also called wild karanda /wild karavanda, referring to the related karanda ("C. carandas").  "Carissa spinarum" is often discussed under its many obsolete synonyms (see below).

List of amphibians of Sri Lanka | Sri Lanka is host to over 110+ species of amphibians, of which over 90 species is being endemic to the country.  The 85% of endemicity ratio makes Sri Lanka the country which has the highest amphibian endemism in Asia.  During the past decade the finding of new amphibians in Sri Lanka has increased significantly.  The first amphibian review in Sri Lanka in 1957 identified 35 species.  In 1996 the number of amphibian species has raised to 53 based on research of museum collections and also a field survey.  More than 250 species were proposed based on this field survey by Pethiyagoda and Manamendra-Arachchi in 1998.  However Meegaskumbura "et al." revised the number to around 140 species, and the discovery of over "new" 100 species has been criticised.  As at 2016, 120 descriptions of amphibian species have been published, with 110 endemics.  Three caecilian species has been identified with one undescribed species.
Carissa Capobianco | Carissa Capobianco (born July 5, 1988) is an American actress.

Carissa (Spain) | Carisa or Carissa (Greek: Κάρισσα , Ptol.  ii.  4, § 13) was an ancient city of the Turdetani in Hispania Baetica, in the "conventus" of Gades, having the "civitas Latina".  Several of its coins are extant.  Pliny refers to the city as Carissa "cognomine" Aurelia.  The city is usually associated, due to inscriptions, with Carixa, near Bornos, in the Province of Cadiz.
Metriochroa carissae | Metriochroa carissae is a moth of the Gracillariidae family.  It is known from Ethiopia.
visible_agent_inputs.current_output: Carissa — about 100 species in the past — had more species than Aruncus, which had only 1–4 species.
